# fig29_wave_km — Emigration Wave KM Survival Curves

**Figure number:** 29
**File:** `charts/fig29_wave_km.png` + `charts/fig29_interactive.html`
**Generated by:** `stage9_wave_disaggregation.py`
**Stage:** Stage 9 — Emigration Wave Disaggregation (V2.5)

## What It Shows

Kaplan–Meier survival curves for three emigration waves (WAVE1, WAVE2, WAVE3) plotted against the non-migrant reference group. Each curve shows the proportion of each wave surviving to each age.

## Key Findings Visible in Chart

- **Wave 3** (Cold War emigration, orange/purple line) shows consistently higher survival than non-migrants — largest gap
- **Wave 2** (WWII displacement, orange line) tracks closely with the non-migrant reference — near-zero gap
- **Wave 1** (UNR/Civil War flight, green line) shows modest survival advantage
- The ranking (Wave 3 > Wave 1 > non-migrant ≈ Wave 2) argues against a simple positive-selection story

## Data

| Wave | n | Mean age at death | Gap vs non-mig | p-value |
|------|---|-------------------|----------------|---------|
| Non-migrated | 6,030 | 71.22 | — | — |
| Wave 1 (pre-1922) | 212 | 73.62 | +2.41 | 0.0002 |
| Wave 2 (1939–45) | 203 | 70.34 | −0.88 | 0.63 |
| Wave 3 (1946–91) | 632 | 75.66 | +4.44 | <0.001 |

## Classification Method

Rule-based extraction from `migration_reasoning` (English text, 78.5% year coverage) with fallback to `notes` (Ukrainian ESU text). UNKNOWN rate: 5.0%.

## Paper Reference

§5.1 — "Wave disaggregation as a structural test"
